sentence_reviews.text: Tokenization and serialization convert language, code, images, audio, actions, or records into packed sequences that model runtimes can consume.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML preprocessing discrete/text data, word embeddings, language models, and vector quantization; AIMA language models, lexicon/grammar, word representations, and languages/algorithms; Deep Learning sequence/model input background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Text/language tokenization and discrete inputs are supported. Code, audio, actions, records, packing, and model runtimes are broader than the inspected packet evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Tokenization, serialization & packing is to connect Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units, Information theory to downstream capabilities including Large language models.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML information theory, preprocessing discrete/text data, word embeddings, and language models; AIMA language models, word representations, and masked/pretrained language-model topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Tokens/vocabularies, information-theoretic language-model quantities, and language-model downstream relevance are supported; exact graph-role wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML language models and word embeddings; AIMA language models, word representations, pretrained contextual representations, and masked language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Token-like language representations are supported as foundational to language models. The source does not specifically use the modern LLM phrase.

sentence_reviews.text: combined with Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML preprocessing discrete/text data and word embeddings; AIMA lexicon/grammar, word representations, and language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Tokenization directly concerns tokens and vocabularies; wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units, Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML information theory and discrete/text preprocessing; AIMA language-model and word-representation topics; Deep Learning methodology/hyperparameter context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Token/information-theory dependencies and some hyperparameter context are supported. The generic limitations are not directly evidenced for tokenization/serialization.

sentence_reviews.text: Tokenization, serialization & packing uses Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML preprocessing discrete/text data and word embeddings; AIMA lexicon/grammar, word representations, and language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: This dependency is directly aligned with tokenization and vocabularies, though the exact rationale phrasing is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Tokenization, serialization & packing uses Information theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML information theory chapter, including entropy, cross entropy, KL divergence, mutual information, and perplexity, plus language-model topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Information-theoretic quantities support vocabulary/language-model evaluation and compression-like framing; the dependency is synthesized rather than directly stated.

sentence_reviews.text: Tokenization, serialization & packing uses Algorithms & data structures as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA Appendix B languages and algorithms, plus language-model and grammar/lexicon topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Algorithms and formal languages are relevant to tokenization/serialization, but the cited evidence does not directly establish the dependency.

edge_reviews.notes: Tokenization depends directly on token/vocabulary concepts; core strength is defensible.
Information theory is relevant to coding, vocabulary efficiency, and language-model quantities, but core strength is a synthesis.
Algorithms and data structures are implementation-relevant, but the inspected evidence does not directly support this as a conceptual dependency. Common strength is plausible.
